notes: One-time expenses can now be logged on the Journal page (for current/past months) as REAL transactions, while Edit-inputs remains for future/projected one-offs — and both surface as a single 'One-time expenses' row on the projection sheet. DESIGN: a journal one-time expense is a normal expense transaction with a reserved category 'oneTime' plus an account, amount, date and a required note (the expense name). Being a standard expense transaction, it debits the chosen account through existing reconciliation (savings chain + salary sweep apply type:'expense' debits; other accounts to be runtime-verified). DEDUP (critical): the reserved 'oneTime' category is routed OUT of the category-actuals map into a separate oneTimeActualByMonth/oneTimeActualTotal, so it never folds into a normal category or the past-month 'sum of all categories'. It is added back exactly once — into the projection sheet's past/current overlay Total Expenses and into the combined One-time row. Journal one-times are NOT stored in state.expenseOneOffs, so the engine's p.surplus (which feeds projectSavingsChain) never sees them; combined with the past-month reverse-walk already applying the account debit via the live balance, there is no double-count path. Current-month savings-chain still uses planned surplus (the pre-existing deferred item), so a current-month one-time debit shows in Total Expenses / sheet surplus but does not re-forecast the forward savings chain — consistent with all other current-month logged expenses. SINGLE ROW: the projection 'One-time expenses' row merges planned one-offs (expandSingleOneOff over state.expenseOneOffs) and journal one-time actuals (oneTimeActualByMonth) per month; the detail popover lists names from whichever source applies. EDIT-INPUTS SCOPE: the one-off list there now shows only future-dated or recurring entries; a hint directs past/current logging to the Journal. Legacy past/current non-recurring planned one-offs remain in state and keep counting via the planned path (user chose 'leave as-is'); saveStep(3) re-attaches these hidden entries so they are never dropped on save. UI: the expense entry modal gains a 'One-time expense' chip (expense type only); selecting it requires a Note. Journal list labels the reserved category as 'One-time expense'. SCOPE NOTE: engine net-worth and projectSavingsChain math untouched; changes are in the projection-sheet overlay/row, the journal modal, and edit-inputs. SELF-TESTS to run in console after adding one journal one-time expense (see chat). KNOWN ITEM (still deferred): current-month savings forecast uses planned surplus. NOTE carried forward: Dashboard Liquidity-health card uses p.cashSeries vs hero using chain cash. SELF_TEST.js and PROPAGATION_TEST_{1,2,3}.js remain valid.
